message.content:
I need to build an endpoint-to-scope mapping for the Google Meet REST API. The Google Discovery document at https://www.googleapis.com/discovery/v1/apis/meet/v2/rest returned 404.

Please research the Google Meet REST API endpoints and their OAuth scopes. Try these approaches:
1. Search the web for "Google Meet REST API reference" and "Google Meet API OAuth scopes"
2. Try fetching https://meet.googleapis.com/$discovery/rest?version=v2
3. Check the Google API documentation

I need the output as a JSON object following this structure:
```json
{
  "provider": "googlemeet",
  "apiHost": "meet.googleapis.com",
  "basePath": "/v2",
  "scopeMap": [
    {
      "method": "GET|POST|PUT|PATCH|DELETE",
      "pathPattern": "/v2/path/with/*/wildcards",
      "sufficientScopes": ["scope1", "scope2"],
      "description": "What this endpoint does"
    }
  ],
  "allScopes": ["scope1", "scope2"]
}
```

Scopes should be shortened (e.g., remove "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/" prefix, just use the short form like "meetings.space.created").

Write the result to /workspace/scope-maps/googlemeet.json

content.text: I'll research the Google Meet REST API endpoints and their OAuth scopes using multiple approaches.

todos.content: Search web for Google Meet REST API reference and OAuth scopes
todos.activeForm: Searching web for Google Meet REST API reference and OAuth scopes

todos.content: Compile endpoint-to-scope mapping and write JSON file
todos.activeForm: Compiling endpoint-to-scope mapping and writing JSON file

input.query: Google Meet REST API v2 reference endpoints OAuth scopes 2025
Google Meet API OAuth scopes meetings.space.created meetings.space.readonly

input.prompt: Extract all API endpoints (methods, paths, descriptions) and their required OAuth scopes from this Google Meet API discovery document. List every endpoint with its HTTP method, path, scopes, and description.

content.content:
# Google Meet API v2 Endpoints

## Conference Records

### Get Conference Record
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecordsId}`
- **Description:** "Gets a conference record by conference ID."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

### List Conference Records
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords`
- **Description:** "Lists the conference records. By default, ordered by start time and in descending order."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

## Recordings

### Get Recording
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecordsId}/recordings/{recordingsId}`
- **Description:** "Gets a recording by recording ID."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

### List Recordings
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecordsId}/recordings`
- **Description:** "Lists the recording resources from the conference record. By default, ordered by start time and in ascending order."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

## Participants

### Get Participant
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecordsId}/participants/{participantsId}`
- **Description:** "Gets a participant by participant ID."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

### List Participants
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecordsId}/participants`
- **Description:** "Lists the participants in a conference record. By default, ordered by join time and in descending order."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

## Participant Sessions

### Get Participant Session
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecordsId}/participants/{participantsId}/participantSessions/{participantSessionsId}`
- **Description:** "Gets a participant session by participant session ID."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

### List Participant Sessions
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecordsId}/participants/{participantsId}/participantSessions`
- **Description:** "Lists the participant sessions of a participant in a conference record. By default, ordered by join time and in descending order."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

## Transcripts

### Get Transcript
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecordsId}/transcripts/{transcriptsId}`
- **Description:** "Gets a transcript by transcript ID."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

### List Transcripts
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecordsId}/transcripts`
- **Description:** "Lists the set of transcripts from the conference record. By default, ordered by start time and in ascending order."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

## Transcript Entries

### Get Transcript Entry
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecordsId}/transcripts/{transcriptsId}/entries/{entriesId}`
- **Description:** "Gets a TranscriptEntry resource by entry ID."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

### List Transcript Entries
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecordsId}/transcripts/{transcriptsId}/entries`
- **Description:** "Lists the structured transcript entries per transcript. By default, ordered by start time and in ascending order."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

## Spaces

### Create Space
- **Method:** POST
- **Path:** `v2/spaces`
- **Description:** "Creates a space."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`

### Get Space
- **Method:** GET
- **Path:** `v2/spaces/{spacesId}`
- **Description:** "Gets details about a meeting space."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.settings`

### Update Space
- **Method:** PATCH
- **Path:** `v2/spaces/{spacesId}`
- **Description:** "Updates details about a meeting space."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.settings`

### End Active Conference
- **Method:** POST
- **Path:** `v2/spaces/{spacesId}:endActiveConference`
- **Description:** "Ends an active conference (if there's one)."
- **Scopes:**
  - `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`

Here's a summary of the Google Meet API OAuth scopes related to **`meetings.space.created`** and **`meetings.space.readonly`**:

## Google Meet API OAuth Scopes

### `meetings.space.created`
`https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created` — Allows apps to create, modify, and read metadata about meeting spaces created by your app.

### `meetings.space.readonly`
`https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly` — Allows apps to read metadata about any meeting space the user has access to.

### Key Differences
- The `meetings.space.created` scope is limited to meeting spaces created by the specific app/project. As observed by users, they could only get space info about Meets created by their specific instance.
- The `meetings.space.readonly` scope provides broader read access to any meeting space the user has access to.

### Additional Scopes
There are also other related scopes:
- `meetings.space.settings` OAuth scope is required for configuring auto artifacts when creating or modifying a meeting space.

### Where These Scopes Are Required

These scopes are required across many Google Meet REST API endpoints:

1. **`spaces.get`** — Requires one of the following OAuth scopes: `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly`, or `meetings.space.settings`.

2. **`conferenceRecords.participants.list`** — Requires one of the following OAuth scopes: `meetings.space.created` or `meetings.space.readonly`.

3. **`conferenceRecords.transcripts.get`** — Requires one of the following OAuth scopes: `meetings.space.created` or `meetings.space.readonly`.

4. **`conferenceRecords.participants.participantSessions.list`** — Requires one of the following OAuth scopes: `meetings.space.created` or `meetings.space.readonly`.

5. **Conference Records (list)** — Authorization is required using OAuth scopes such as `meetings.space.created` or `meetings.space.readonly`.

### Best Practices

Generally, you should choose the most narrowly focused scope possible and avoid requesting scopes that your app doesn't require. Users more readily grant access to limited, clearly described scopes.

### Authentication Methods

If you're a domain administrator, you can grant domain-wide delegation of authority to authorize an application's service account to access your users' data without requiring each user to give consent. After you configure domain-wide delegation, the service account can impersonate a user account, which is considered user authentication.

For more details, see the official [Google Meet API Authentication & Authorization guide](https://developers.google.com/workspace/meet/api/guides/authenticate-authorize).

Here's a summary of the **Google Meet REST API v2** — its reference endpoints, OAuth scopes, and related information for 2025:

---

## General Availability

V2 of the Google Meet API is now generally available, with full v2 reference documentation available. The service discovery endpoint is `https://meet.googleapis.com/$discovery/rest?version=v2`, and the service has a base service endpoint with all URIs relative to it.

---

## Key REST API v2 Endpoints

### Spaces
- **GET** `https://meet.googleapis.com/v2/{name=spaces/*}` — Retrieves a meeting space.
- Use the **`/v2/spaces`** endpoint with your auth headers to create a Google Meet link programmatically.

### Conference Records — Transcript Entries
- **GET** `https://meet.googleapis.com/v2/{name=conferenceRecords/*/transcripts/*/entries/*}` — Gets a specific transcript entry.
- **GET** `https://meet.googleapis.com/v2/{parent=conferenceRecords/*/transcripts/*}/entries` — Lists transcript entries for a conference record.

---

## OAuth 2.0 Scopes

Authorization scopes are the permissions you request users to authorize for your app to access meeting content. When someone installs your app, the user is asked to validate these scopes. You should choose the most narrowly focused scope possible and avoid requesting scopes your app doesn't require.

The Meet REST API supports the following **OAuth 2.0 scopes**:

| Scope | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly` | Read-only access to meeting spaces |
| `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created` | Access to spaces created by the app |
| `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.settings` | Access to meeting space settings (e.g., auto-recording) |
| `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.readonly` | Recordings access (this is a Google Drive scope) |

### Scope Requirements by Endpoint

- **`spaces.get`**: Requires one of the following OAuth scopes: `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly`, or `meetings.space.settings`.
- **Transcript entries (get/list)**: Requires one of the following OAuth scopes: `meetings.space.created` or `meetings.space.readonly`.

### Scope Sensitivity

The "Usage" column in the official docs indicates the sensitivity of each scope — **Non-sensitive** scopes provide the smallest scope of authorization access and only require basic app verification. **Sensitive** scopes provide access to specific Google user data that's authorized by the user for the app.

---

## Authentication

For the Meet REST API, you can only authenticate using user authentication. This uses OAuth 2.0 with a user's Google credentials, which lets Meet apps access user data and perform operations on the authenticated user's behalf, with the same permissions as that user.

### Domain-Wide Delegation

If you're a domain administrator, you can grant domain-wide delegation of authority to authorize an application's service account to access your users' data without requiring each user to give consent. After configuring this, the service account can impersonate a user account — though it is still considered user authentication, and any capability that requires user authentication can use domain-wide delegation.

---

## Important Notes for 2025

- The Google Workspace Events API v1beta endpoint will be decommissioned for Google Chat and Google Meet events on April 30, 2025. To subscribe to Chat or Meet events, use the generally available v1 endpoint.
- At minimum, you'll need the `meetings.space` scope for creating and managing meetings. If you want recordings or transcripts, add `drive.readonly` for access to stored files.

For the full and most up-to-date reference, visit the official documentation at:
- **API Reference**: https://developers.google.com/workspace/meet/api/reference/rest/v2
- **Auth & Scopes Guide**: https://developers.google.com/workspace/meet/api/guides/authenticate-authorize

input.prompt: List ALL resources and methods available in the Google Meet REST API v2. I need every endpoint - spaces, conferenceRecords, participants, participantSessions, recordings, transcripts, transcript entries, members, and any others. List the HTTP method, path, description, and required OAuth scopes for each.

input.query: Google Meet REST API v2 "spaces.members" endpoint create delete list member

content.content:
# Google Meet REST API v2 - Complete Resource & Method List

Based on the documentation provided, here are all available endpoints in the Google Meet REST API v2:

## REST Resources and Methods

### v2.spaces
- **POST** `/v2/spaces` — Creates a space
- **GET** `/v2/{name=spaces/*}` — Gets details about a meeting space
- **PATCH** `/v2/{space.name=spaces/*}` — Updates details about a meeting space
- **POST** `/v2/{name=spaces/*}:endActiveConference` — Ends an active conference

### v2.conferenceRecords
- **GET** `/v2/{name=conferenceRecords/*}` — Gets a conference record by conference ID
- **GET** `/v2/conferenceRecords` — Lists the conference records

### v2.conferenceRecords.participants
- **GET** `/v2/{name=conferenceRecords/*/participants/*}` — Gets a participant by participant ID
- **GET** `/v2/{parent=conferenceRecords/*}/participants` — Lists participants in a conference record

### v2.conferenceRecords.participants.participantSessions
- **GET** `/v2/{name=conferenceRecords/*/participants/*/participantSessions/*}` — Gets a participant session by ID
- **GET** `/v2/{parent=conferenceRecords/*/participants/*}/participantSessions` — Lists participant sessions

### v2.conferenceRecords.recordings
- **GET** `/v2/{name=conferenceRecords/*/recordings/*}` — Gets a recording by recording ID
- **GET** `/v2/{parent=conferenceRecords/*}/recordings` — Lists recording resources from conference record

### v2.conferenceRecords.transcripts
- **GET** `/v2/{name=conferenceRecords/*/transcripts/*}` — Gets a transcript by transcript ID
- **GET** `/v2/{parent=conferenceRecords/*}/transcripts` — Lists transcripts from conference record

### v2.conferenceRecords.transcripts.entries
- **GET** `/v2/{name=conferenceRecords/*/transcripts/*/entries/*}` — Gets a TranscriptEntry by ID
- **GET** `/v2/{parent=conferenceRecords/*/transcripts/*}/entries` — Lists structured transcript entries

**Service Endpoint:** `https://meet.googleapis.com`

**Note:** OAuth scopes are not detailed in the provided documentation excerpt.

Here's a comprehensive overview of the Google Meet REST API `spaces.members` endpoint:

## Google Meet REST API — `spaces.members` Endpoint

### Overview

This feature is available as part of the **Google Workspace Developer Preview Program**, which grants early access to certain features.

A space member is a user who's configured to have a role in the meeting space. These users can join the meeting space without requesting permission to connect (also known as "knocking"). Additionally, members can be configured to have a **Role** in the meeting, such as `COHOST` which gives them the same abilities to manage the meeting as the meeting organizer.

### Resource Fields

The `spaces.members` resource includes the **name**, **user**, and **role** fields.

### API Endpoint

To call these methods, use the `/v2beta/` URL endpoint. For example, the HTTP request for the `spaces.members.list` method becomes `https://meet.googleapis.com/v2beta/spaces/{space}/members`.

### Available Methods

The `spaces.members` resource supports four methods: **create**, **get**, **list**, and **delete**.

Each method takes a path parameter with the **create** and **list** methods using the `parent` field to denote the meeting space, and the **delete** and **get** methods using the `name` field to filter the results based on the meeting space and the member name.

#### 1. **Create Member** (`spaces.members.create`)
Developer Preview: Create a member. This API supports the `fields` parameter in SystemParameterContext. When the `fields` parameter is omitted, this API response will default to `"name,email,role,user"`.
- **Parent format:** `spaces/{space}`
- Returns an instance of the `Member` resource.

#### 2. **Get Member** (`spaces.members.get`)
Developer Preview: Get a member. This API supports the `fields` parameter in SystemParameterContext. When the `fields` parameter is omitted, this API response will default to `"name,email,role,user"`.
- **Name format:** `spaces/{space}/members/{member}`

#### 3. **List Members** (`spaces.members.list`)
Developer Preview: List members. This API supports the `fields` parameter in SystemParameterContext. When the `fields` parameter is omitted this API response will default to `"name,email,role,user"`.
- **Parent format:** `spaces/{space}`

#### 4. **Delete Member** (`spaces.members.delete`)
Developer Preview: Delete the member who was previously assigned roles in the space.
- **Name format:** `spaces/{space}/members/{member}`

### Response Behavior

All methods, except delete, return an instance of the `spaces.members` resource.

All methods, except delete, support the `fields` parameter in SystemParameterContext. When the `fields` parameter is omitted, the API response defaults to `"name,email,role,user"`. The `fields` parameter relies on a FieldMask for response filtering. A FieldMask is a way for API callers to list fields a request should return. It's a comma-delimited list of fields to specify only certain fields in an object are used while leaving the other fields unchanged.

### Relationship to Space Configuration

While meeting organizers can automatically enter the meeting space, you can also configure additional members to join the meeting without knocking.

If an explicit role is set for a user in `spaces.members`, the user joins as that role.

### Client Library Support

The `spaces.members` methods are available in the **v2beta** client libraries across multiple languages including:

- Go (`apiv2beta`): The `SpacesCallOptions` struct includes `CreateMember`, `GetMember`, `ListMembers`, and `DeleteMember` call options.
- PHP (`V2beta`): Available through the `SpacesServiceClient` class with methods like `createMember`, `getMember`, `listMembers`, and `deleteMember`.

### Key Notes

- These endpoints are currently in **Developer Preview** and use the **`v2beta`** URL path, not the stable `v2` path.
- The stable `v2` REST resources include `v2.conferenceRecords`, `v2.conferenceRecords.participants`, `v2.conferenceRecords.recordings`, `v2.conferenceRecords.transcripts`, and `v2.spaces` — but `spaces.members` is not yet listed under the stable v2 reference, confirming its Developer Preview status.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Good - the `spaces.members` endpoints are v2beta only (Developer Preview), not part of the stable v2 API. Let me now verify individual endpoint scopes by checking a few specific endpoint documentation pages.

input.prompt: List ALL OAuth scopes for the Google Meet REST API. Include the full scope URL, the short name, the description, and whether they are sensitive/restricted. I need a complete list of every scope.
What are the required OAuth scopes for the spaces.create endpoint? List the HTTP method, path, and all required scopes.
What are the required OAuth scopes for the spaces.patch endpoint? List the HTTP method, path, and all required scopes.

content.content:
# Google Meet spaces.create Endpoint

**HTTP Method:** POST

**Path:** `https://meet.googleapis.com/v2/spaces`

**Required OAuth Scope:**
- `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`

This single scope is necessary to authorize applications creating new meeting spaces through the Google Meet API.

# spaces.patch Endpoint Requirements

**HTTP Method:** PATCH

**Path:** `https://meet.googleapis.com/v2/{space.name=spaces/*}`

**Required OAuth Scopes:**
1. `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
2. `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.settings`

This endpoint allows you to "update details about a meeting space" and requires one of these two scopes for authorization.

# Google Meet REST API OAuth Scopes

Based on the documentation, here are all the OAuth scopes for the Google Meet REST API:

## Primary Meet Scopes

| Scope URL | Description | Classification |
|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.settings` | "Edit and see the settings for all of your Google Meet calls." | Non-sensitive |
| `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created` | "Allow apps to create, modify, and read metadata about meeting spaces created by your app." | Sensitive |
| `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly` | "Allow apps to read metadata about any meeting space the user has access to." | Sensitive |
| `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.readonly` | "Allow apps to download recording and transcript files from Google Drive API." | Restricted |

## Related Google Drive Scope

| Scope URL | Description | Classification |
|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.meet.readonly` | "View Drive files created or edited by Google Meet." | Restricted |

**Note:** The documentation indicates developers may add scopes from other Google APIs as needed. The classifications reflect different verification requirements, with non-sensitive scopes requiring basic verification, sensitive scopes requiring additional verification, and restricted scopes requiring the most stringent review processes.

input.prompt: What are the required OAuth scopes for the spaces.endActiveConference endpoint? List the HTTP method, path, and all required scopes.
What are the required OAuth scopes for the conferenceRecords.list endpoint? List the HTTP method, path, and all required scopes.
What are the required OAuth scopes for the conferenceRecords.recordings.get endpoint? List the HTTP method, path, and all required scopes.

content.content:
# spaces.endActiveConference OAuth Requirements

**HTTP Method:** POST

**Path:** `https://meet.googleapis.com/v2/{name=spaces/*}:endActiveConference`

**Required OAuth Scope:**
- `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`

This single scope is necessary to authorize requests that end an active conference within a meeting space.

# conferenceRecords.list OAuth Requirements

**HTTP Method & Path:**
- GET `https://meet.googleapis.com/v2/conferenceRecords`

**Required OAuth Scopes:**

The endpoint requires one of the following scopes:

1. `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
2. `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

These scopes grant permission to access meeting space data, with the second option providing read-only access to existing spaces.

# conferenceRecords.recordings.get Endpoint

**HTTP Method:** GET

**Path:** `https://meet.googleapis.com/v2/{name=conferenceRecords/*/recordings/*}`

**Required OAuth Scopes:**

1. `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created`
2. `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly`

According to the documentation, you must provide "one of the following OAuth scopes" to authenticate this request, which retrieves a recording by its ID from Google Meet conference records.

content.text:
The file has been written to `/workspace/scope-maps/googlemeet.json`. Here is a summary of what was found and compiled.

---

## Research Methodology

1. **Web search** for "Google Meet REST API v2 reference" and "Google Meet API OAuth scopes" -- found official documentation links.
2. **Discovery document** at `https://meet.googleapis.com/$discovery/rest?version=v2` -- this worked and returned the complete machine-readable API specification with all endpoints, methods, paths, and scopes.
3. **Individual endpoint pages** on `developers.google.com` -- cross-verified the scopes for `spaces.create`, `spaces.patch`, `spaces.endActiveConference`, `conferenceRecords.list`, and `conferenceRecords.recordings.get`.
4. **Authentication/authorization guide** at `developers.google.com/workspace/meet/api/guides/authenticate-authorize` -- confirmed the complete list of OAuth scopes.

## Endpoints Found (16 total, stable v2 API)

### Spaces (4 endpoints)
| Method | Path | Scopes |
|--------|------|--------|
| POST | `/v2/spaces` | `meetings.space.created` |
| GET | `/v2/spaces/*` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly`, `meetings.space.settings` |
| PATCH | `/v2/spaces/*` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.settings` |
| POST | `/v2/spaces/*:endActiveConference` | `meetings.space.created` |

### Conference Records (2 endpoints)
| Method | Path | Scopes |
|--------|------|--------|
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords/*` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |

### Participants (2 endpoints)
| Method | Path | Scopes |
|--------|------|--------|
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords/*/participants` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords/*/participants/*` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |

### Participant Sessions (2 endpoints)
| Method | Path | Scopes |
|--------|------|--------|
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords/*/participants/*/participantSessions` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords/*/participants/*/participantSessions/*` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |

### Recordings (2 endpoints)
| Method | Path | Scopes |
|--------|------|--------|
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords/*/recordings` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords/*/recordings/*` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |

### Transcripts (2 endpoints)
| Method | Path | Scopes |
|--------|------|--------|
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords/*/transcripts` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords/*/transcripts/*` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |

### Transcript Entries (2 endpoints)
| Method | Path | Scopes |
|--------|------|--------|
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords/*/transcripts/*/entries` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |
| GET | `/v2/conferenceRecords/*/transcripts/*/entries/*` | `meetings.space.created`, `meetings.space.readonly` |

## OAuth Scopes (3 Meet-specific scopes)

| Short Name | Full URL | Sensitivity | Description |
|------------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `meetings.space.created` | `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.created` | Sensitive | Create, modify, and read metadata about meeting spaces created by your app |
| `meetings.space.readonly` | `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.readonly` | Sensitive | Read metadata about any meeting space the user has access to |
| `meetings.space.settings` | `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.settings` | Non-sensitive | Edit and see the settings for all Google Meet calls |

## Notable Exclusions

- **`spaces.members`** (create, get, list, delete) -- These endpoints exist only in the **v2beta** API and are part of the Developer Preview Program. They were intentionally excluded from this mapping since the scope map targets the stable v2 API.
- **`drive.readonly`** and **`drive.meet.readonly`** -- These are Google Drive scopes used for downloading actual recording/transcript files from Drive, not for the Meet API itself. They were excluded from `allScopes` since they belong to the Drive API, not the Meet API.

Sources:
- [Google Meet API v2 Reference](https://developers.google.com/workspace/meet/api/reference/rest/v2)
- [Authenticate and authorize Meet REST API requests](https://developers.google.com/workspace/meet/api/guides/authenticate-authorize)
- [Configure meeting spaces and members](https://developers.google.com/workspace/meet/api/guides/meeting-spaces-configuration)
- [Method: spaces.get](https://developers.google.com/workspace/meet/api/reference/rest/v2/spaces/get)
- [Method: spaces.create](https://developers.google.com/workspace/meet/api/reference/rest/v2/spaces/create)
